items.title: Rising petrol costs drive surge in tricycle sales, bike repairs

children.content: Skyrocketing fuel prices are driving Australians to pedal power, with some bicycle stores and repair services experiencing a surge in demand.

gallery.altText: A woman riding a tricycle outdoors.

thumbnail.altText: A woman riding a tricycle outdoors.

items.title: Teen dies after crashing bike at Canberra's Stromlo Forest Park

children.content: Police say the 14-year-old boy came off his bike while riding in Stromlo Forest Park on Saturday, and later died in hospital.

image.altText: A photo of the forest park with shrubbery, rocks and signs and blue sky in the distance.

items.title: Reefs at risk as warming water sees sea urchins spread further

children.content: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a Senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

image.altText: diver under water with sea urchins

items.title: Varroa mite 'in all corners' of ACT as more than 1,000 beehives destroyed

children.content: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite varroa mite with more than 1,000 destroyed.

gallery.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

thumbnail.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

items.title: 'Grow up': Meta censoring warnings about illicit drugs, health experts say

children.content: Public health workers are warning lives could be at risk due to social media sites automatically censoring educational posts about illicit drugs circulating in the community.

image.altText: a small orange and grey pill next to a ruler

items.title: Man jailed for role in major steroid and prescription drug syndicate

children.content: A Canberra man is jailed for his part in an illegal drug ring which operated as an online pharmacy, distributing steroids and prescription drugs across the country.

image.altText: A box of pills and drugs.

items.title: Mary has fallen into an aged care 'black hole' and sees no way out

children.content: After being stuck in a hospital in Canberra for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

gallery.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

items.title: Singapore's PM knows Australia is vulnerable but Albanese has leverage

children.content: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

image.altText: Two men shake hands in front of two flags

items.title: Top neuroscientist slams AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

gallery.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

thumbnail.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: Uni in financial crisis spent nearly $1.5m on leadership coach, documents show

children.content: Documents released to Four Corners show the University of Technology Sydney spent hundreds of thousands of dollars a year on a leadership coach before planning to slash jobs and courses to save money.

image.altText: A person carrying a bag walks through a university campus. Behind them is a large multi-story glass building.

items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: Edward James Kilbey's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld the 83-year-old's appeal in part due to his "ill health".

image.altText: A man wearing sunglasses on a swing at a children's playground.

items.title: House empty more than a year after 69-year-old renter forced out

children.content: After a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is left battling for a place to call home.

image.altText: An older woman in glasses looks sternly at the camera, as she holds the handle of the front door to a house.

items.title: ASX slips as doubts about US-Iran ceasefire grow

children.content: Australian shares opened lower after climbing yesterday following the US and Iran agreeing to a two-week ceasefire that's now on the brink of collapse.

image.altText: trump nato wtf

items.title: Couple forced to move home accidentally built on wrong block

children.content: A Victorian couple has been forced to move their home that was accidentally built on the wrong block.

gallery.altText: a man and a woman look at the camera with a serious look on their face
house in a field with a blue sky
a home is stacked on oil drums for support

house in a field with a blue sky
a home is stacked on oil drums for support

items.title: Family still waits to know how their loved one died, one year on

children.content: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

image.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

items.title: Racquets smashed, tempers flare as Medvedev suffers 6-0, 6-0 meltdown

children.content: Daniil Medvedev endures an eventful day at the Monte Carlo Masters, smashing several racquets before suffering the first 6-0, 6-0 trouncing of his distinguished career.

image.altText: Male tennis player grunts after hitting a shot during a match

items.title: Comedian Grace Jarvis on most bizarre encounter working in sex shop

children.content: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

gallery.altText: Grace Jarvis smiles while holding a mic and talking on stage.

thumbnail.altText: Grace Jarvis smiles while holding a mic and talking on stage.

items.title: Test your knowledge with our classic Australian TV quiz

children.content: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

image.altText: Ray Meagher on the set of Home and Away playing Alf Stewart.

items.title: Failed referendum inspires two top dance companies to join forces

children.content: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

image.altText: On stage, an Indigenous dancer leans back, stretching one arm up. Behind her other dancers in shadow form a line.

items.title: US architect admits killing eight women in Gilgo Beach murders

children.content: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to killing another woman, solving the notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie smiling in a court room.
Crime scene investigators putting on full-body PPE out the front of a home.
A wide shot of a sandy beach featuring an American flag on a pole, with the ocean in the background.

items.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry

children.content: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

image.altText: A picture of Matthew Perry at a film premiere.

items.title: Brisbane suburbs set to become 'mini CBDs' as city plans for population surge

children.content: As Brisbane prepares for an expected surge in population over the next 15 years, the council has introduced a plan to allow high-rise apartments in some of the city's suburban hubs.

image.altText: A sand coloured block of apartments

items.title: More than 1,000 fish killed in Darwin after iron ore pollution

children.content: More than 1,000 small fish die in Darwin Port after acidic run-off from an iron ore stockpile enters a containment pond following heavy rain.

image.altText: An aerial view of a ship anchored at a wharf part of the Darwin Port.

items.title: Unusable road may be the end of remote community as food supplies dwindle

children.content: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

image.altText: A damaged road with red sand on either side.

items.title: Transit officers to take up arms on Darwin's 'dangerous' bus network

children.content: As the conflict in the Middle East continues, more people than ever are turning to public transport to escape the cost of driving — but in Darwin, that choice comes with a different price.

image.altText: A bus driving in Darwin.

items.title: Faldo says he will not comment on rival Norman until after Masters

children.content: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

image.altText: Nick Faldo and Greg Norman stand together on the tee, in front of a huge gallery of fans

items.title: Controversial fence dividing dog owners and environmentalists removed

children.content: A controversial fence designed to keep dogs out of a park in Melbourne's north has been removed after community opposition.

gallery.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

thumbnail.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

items.title: Cyclone Maila moving again towards Far North Queensland coast

children.content: Cyclone Maila has woken up and is heading west, with the category four system still likely to reach Queensland's far north on Monday.

image.altText: A satellite image of Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila in the Solomon Sea.

items.title: Residents heard gunshots before body found in Melbourne apartment tower

children.content: Homicide squad detectives from Victoria Police are investigating a fatal shooting at a Box Hill apartment building.

image.altText: Forensic services arriving at a Box Hill tower.

items.title: Festival chief warned author's removal would 'decimate' Writers' Week

children.content: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

image.altText: Emails obtained under FOI relating to Adelaide Writers' Week.

items.title: What's in 'Australian Made' products? Not necessarily Australian produce

children.content: Farmers battling cheap imported produce want a review of food labelling laws to highlight what Australian produce is included in products.

image.altText: A farmer amongst his soybean crop.

items.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?

children.content: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

articles.title: Food charities struggle to help as fuel cost crisis bites

children.content: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are adapting their services to meet a surge in demand as more people struggle with rising costs.

image.altText: Boxes full of fruit and vegetables.

articles.title: 'Do we really need to come': Patients question travel for treatment due to fuel prices

children.content: Paige Edgar never imagined the hardest part of her one-year-old son's brain cancer diagnosis would be getting him to treatment five hours from her home.

image.altText: a woman in a field holding her two children

items.title: Mining on the Moon is closer than ever, but challenges remain

children.content: A scientist, an engineer, an archaeologist and a lawyer break down some of the physical, environmental, ethical and legal challenges of mining on the Moon.

image.altText: The surface of the Moon, with Earth rising in the background.

items.title: Early season brilliance puts Gawn on track for record All-Australian nod

children.content: Melbourne captain Max Gawn shows no sign of slowing down as an excellent first month of the season puts him front and centre of ABC Sport's rolling AFL All-Australian team.

image.altText: Max Gawn pokes his tongue out at the camera

items.title: Distance triples and comeback kings end Aus Open swimming

children.content: Sam Williamson capped his comeback with victory in the 50m breaststroke just 11 months after he suffered a career-threatening injury.
